root_cause.summary: argo-cd's `Run unit tests with -race for Go packages` job had three distinct test failures across three packages, surfacing two related root-cause categories. (1) cmd/argocd/commands.TestWaitOnApplicationStatus_JSON_YAML_WideOutput at app_test.go:1936 — a formatted-output assertion failed because the test's expected output captured timestamps at one second BEFORE the actual rendering happened, so `2026-05-08T03:25:36` (expected) vs `2026-05-08T03:25:35` (actual) caused a string-equality mismatch on the wide-output formatter. This is a timing-flake / clock-snapshot bug in the test fixture, not a logic bug in the wait-on-status command itself. (2) server.TestOIDCRefresh — `testing.go:1712: race detected during execution of test` (Go race detector under `go test -race`); the test triggers an OIDC token-refresh path that accesses shared state without locking. (3) util/db.TestClusterRaceConditionClusterSecrets — second `testing.go:1712: race detected`; concurrent-access on cluster secrets storage. The package-level FAIL coverage lines (L61296 cmd/argocd/commands 48.495s, L67598 server 30.793s, L80596 util/db 38.827s) confirm three packages failed. Final `=== Failed` summary at L87473-88904 enumerates all three with file paths and the diff/race traces; ##[error]Process completed with exit code 2 at L88904.

required_signals.value: testing.go:1712: race detected during execution of test

evidence_spans.reason: First ##[group]FAIL marker at ~62% of the log: identifies the cmd/argocd/commands.TestWaitOnApplicationStatus_JSON_YAML_WideOutput failure. This is the EARLIEST failure marker in the log — a tail-200 window starting from the bottom would miss this entirely (it's >34000 lines from the bottom).
Second failure block: ##[group]FAIL server.TestOIDCRefresh + the race-detector trace ~13000 lines later (~74% of log). Multiple-failure-locality marker.
Third failure block: ##[group]FAIL util/db.TestClusterRaceConditionClusterSecrets + race trace at ~85% of log. Spans 1285 lines internally.

Consolidated `=== Failed` summary at the end of the log (~98%) listing all three failures: TestWaitOnApplicationStatus_JSON_YAML_WideOutput with the expected/actual diff at app_test.go:1936, TestOIDCRefresh with race trace, TestClusterRaceConditionClusterSecrets with race trace. ##[error]Process completed with exit code 2 at L88904. This block is the densest single span — a tail-1500 window would catch all three failure summaries in one chunk.
